title: Fiery Tacu Cheese Fritters
description: A spicy twist on traditional Peruvian tacu tacu, these crispy fritters are filled with melted cheese and served with a tangy side of salsa.
instructions: 1. Preheat the oil in a large skillet to 350°F (175°C).
2. In a large bowl, crush the taco shells into fine crumbs.
3. In a separate bowl, mix the cheese, diced pepper, onion, garlic, and egg.
4. Add the cheese mixture to the taco shell crumbs and stir until well combined.
5. Divide the mixture into 8 equal portions and shape each portion into a ball.
6. Flatten each ball slightly into a disk shape.
7. Dip each disk into the breadcrumbs, coating both sides evenly.
8. Fry the fritters in batches until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes per side.
9. Drain the fritters on paper towels and serve hot with a side of aji amarillo sauce.
